description:
Your team’s data-cleaning pipeline relies on the public JSONPlaceholder
    test-API.  Before you begin wrangling the real data you first want to make
    sure the service is alive and returning the shapes you expect.

    Goal
    ----
    1. Run three HTTP requests with curl (two GETs and one POST) to the URLs given
       below.
    2. Do *minimal* content-validation on the replies (see “Rules”).
    3. Write a compact CSV-style log file that summarises the results.

    Allowed tools
    -------------
    • curl  
    • Any standard shell utilities that already exist in the container
      (e.g. jq, grep, awk, sed, wc, etc.).  
    • You do NOT need (and must not attempt) to install extra software.

    Directory layout
    ----------------
    • Create a new directory:
         /home/user/api_checks
    • All artefacts produced by your commands must live **inside that directory**.

    The three HTTP calls you must issue
    -----------------------------------
    STEP 1   (GET)
        URL  : https://jsonplaceholder.typicode.com/posts/1  
        Test : Response JSON must contain the key-value pair  
               "id": 1      (an exact match, whitespace irrelevant).

    STEP 2   (GET)
        URL  : https://jsonplaceholder.typicode.com/comments?postId=1  
        Test : The reply is a JSON array whose length is **exactly 5**.

    STEP 3   (POST)
        URL  : https://jsonplaceholder.typicode.com/posts  
        Body : {"title":"foo","body":"bar","userId":888}  
        Test : The server must return HTTP status 201 and the JSON body must
               contain an integer field called **id** whose value is 101.

    The log file to produce
    -----------------------
    Path:  /home/user/api_checks/api_test_report.log

    Format (exact, case-sensitive, comma-separated, **no leading or trailing
    spaces** on any line):

        step,method,url,status_code,validation_passed
        1,GET,https://jsonplaceholder.typicode.com/posts/1,200,OK
        2,GET,https://jsonplaceholder.typicode.com/comments?postId=1,200,OK
        3,POST,https://jsonplaceholder.typicode.com/posts,201,OK

    • The first line is the fixed header shown above.
    • “status_code” is the numeric code curl observed for that request.
    • “validation_passed” must be exactly the literal string “OK” if your test
      for that row succeeded, otherwise “FAIL”.
    • No blank lines are allowed anywhere in the file.

    What the automated checker will look for
    ----------------------------------------
    1. The file /home/user/api_checks/api_test_report.log exists.
    2. Its contents match *exactly* the 4-line CSV shown above.
    3. File permissions allow read access for the current (non-root) user.

    Extra requirements / hints
    --------------------------
    • Keep your terminal output readable but do not rely on it for grading;
      only the contents of api_test_report.log will be assessed.
    • Remember to create the /home/user/api_checks directory before writing the
      log file, otherwise the write will fail.
    • Use the `-s` (silent) flag in curl if you want to suppress progress bars.
    • You may find jq useful for JSON parsing, but any method that yields the
      correct validation logic is acceptable.

    You should need no more than 3-4 shell commands in total to complete the
    assignment.

    When you’re done, double-check the file location and its content—they must
    match the specification byte-for-byte.
truth:
Expected absolute path of the log file:
        /home/user/api_checks/api_test_report.log

    Exact byte-for-byte content the grader will look for (including newlines):

        step,method,url,status_code,validation_passed\n
        1,GET,https://jsonplaceholder.typicode.com/posts/1,200,OK\n
        2,GET,https://jsonplaceholder.typicode.com/comments?postId=1,200,OK\n
        3,POST,https://jsonplaceholder.typicode.com/posts,201,OK\n

    Notes for grader:
        • The file must have exactly four lines, each terminated by a single '\n'.
        • Leading/trailing whitespace is NOT allowed.
        • Permissions: rw-r--r-- (0644) or more permissive is acceptable.
